_description: Gold dataset: Probate lead research — expected Research Agent output for a Spokane County probate filing.
_note: Populate with real lead data after first production run. This is the structure template.

input.operatorNotes: Probate filing found on county docket. Need to confirm PR/executor and property condition.

artifacts.sourceLabel: Spokane County Superior Court — Probate Docket
artifacts.extractedNotes: Case #26-4-00123-32. Filed 2025-11-12. Decedent: John Doe. Personal Representative: Jane Doe (daughter). Hearing date: 2026-02-15.
Property assessed at $275,000. Last sale 2001 for $142,000. Single family, 3bd/2ba, 1,650 sqft. Built 1972.

facts.factValue: Probate filed November 2025, case #26-4-00123-32. Hearing Feb 2026.
Jane Doe (daughter) is Personal Representative / Executor
Built 1972, 3bd/2ba, 1,650 sqft. No recent permits suggesting deferred maintenance.

dossier.situationSummary: Probate property — John Doe passed away, daughter Jane Doe is the executor (case #26-4-00123-32). Property has been in the family since 2001, likely needs updates given 1972 build with no recent permits.
dossier.recommendedCallAngle: Call as a local cash buyer who works with probate families. Mention awareness of the probate timeline. Ask about Jane's plans for the property and whether the estate needs a quick resolution. Emphasize no repairs needed, no commissions, and flexibility on closing timeline.

topFacts.value: 1972 build, no recent permits, likely deferred maintenance

verificationChecklist.item: Verify probate case number and status on county website
Confirm Jane Doe is still PR (no substitution)
Check for any liens or encumbrances
Verify property is not occupied by other heirs
